question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the ordering of the signatures collection cause validate() to derive totalStake exclusively from the first signature's node via getTotalStake(), allowing a crafted low-totalStake node placed first to artificially lower stakeRequiredForConsensus and make a minority-stake hash group falsely reach consensus, causing illegitimate transactions to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a signature object whose node reports a fraudulently small getTotalStake() value (e.g., 1) as the first element in the collection, so that getStakeRequiredForConsensus() computes a threshold of 1 tinybar, allowing a single low-stake node's VERIFIED signature to satisfy canReachConsensus() and gossip a forged transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where multiple nodes report inconsistent getTotalStake() values, and because validate() only reads totalStake from the first node without cross-validating against remaining nodes, cause the consensus threshold to be computed from a manipulated value, leading to acceptance of a transaction that lacks genuine network consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signatures collection where getFileHashAsHex() returns null for all VERIFIED signatures (by supplying null fileHash bytes), causing signatureHashMap.put(null, signature) to group all null-hash signatures under a single null key, and if their combined stake meets stakeRequiredForConsensus, falsely mark all those signatures as CONSENSUS_REACHED and accept a fabricated stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals() check at line 54 by ensuring consensusRatio is set to a BigDecimal with a non-zero scale (e.g., new BigDecimal('0.0') which does NOT equal BigDecimal.ZERO under .equals()) to bypass the zero-ratio early-return path, or conversely to trigger it unexpectedly, causing the consensus check to be silently skipped and any VERIFIED signature to be treated as sufficient for accepting a transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by supplying many VERIFIED signatures whose individual getStake() values sum beyond Long.MAX_VALUE, wrapping the accumulated stake to a negative value, causing canReachConsensus() to return false even when legitimate consensus exists, thereby suppressing valid transactions (denial of service)?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the same node identity appears multiple times with VERIFIED status and the same fileHashAsHex, causing HashMultimap to store duplicate entries under the same key, inflating the computed stake sum for that hash group beyond the actual network stake held by that node, and thereby satisfying canReachConsensus() with a minority of real stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusCount is incremented by validatedSignatures.size() (a count of signatures, not stake) and the only check is consensusCount > 0, meaning a single hash group with even one signature reaching canReachConsensus() causes validate() to return successfully, to craft a scenario where a single high-stake compromised node's signature alone passes consensus and gossips a fraudulent transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the SignatureVerificationException('Invalid total staking weight') path in getStakeRequiredForConsensus() by injecting a signature whose node returns getTotalStake() == 0 as the first element, causing validate() to throw before any consensus check occurs, effectively creating a denial-of-service that prevents any stream file from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the stakeRequiredForConsensus BigDecimal (computed with RoundingMode.CEILING) by supplying a totalStake value that, when multiplied by consensusRatio and rounded up, equals exactly the stake of a single attacker-controlled node, allowing that single node to satisfy canReachConsensus() and unilaterally accept a transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all VERIFIED signatures share the same fileHashAsHex (attacker-controlled hash) but represent nodes with zero getStake() values, and because the zero-stake fallback in ConsensusNodeWrapper assigns stake=1 per node, cause the stake sum to meet stakeRequiredForConsensus computed from a nodeCount-based totalStake, falsely reaching consensus on a forged file hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signatures collection with two competing hash groups where the minority group (attacker-controlled) is iterated first by HashMultimap's non-deterministic keySet() ordering, and if that group happens to meet canReachConsensus(), validate() returns immediately after setting CONSENSUS_REACHED on the attacker's signatures, causing the downstream Downloader to fetch and process the attacker's fraudulent stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of cross-validation between filename values across signatures (filename is set only from the first signature and never compared against others) to inject signatures with mismatched filenames, causing the consensus check to pass on a hash group that corresponds to a different file than the one being validated, leading to acceptance of a wrong transaction batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where only non-VERIFIED statuses (DOWNLOADED, NOT_FOUND, CONSENSUS_REACHED) are present, causing signatureHashMap to remain empty, and then if consensusRatio is exactly BigDecimal.ZERO (scale 0), the early-return condition at line 54 requires signatureHashMap to be non-empty, so the check fails and a SignatureVerificationException is thrown — can this be used to selectively block processing of specific legitimate files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the node stake data (e.g., via a stale NodeStakeRepository) cause getTotalStake() to return an outdated value that is much smaller than the real network stake, lowering stakeRequiredForConsensus to a value achievable by a minority coalition of nodes, allowing those nodes' VERIFIED signatures to falsely reach consensus and gossip unauthorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a VERIFIED signature with a fileHash that is an empty byte array (length 0), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string '', grouping all such signatures under the '' key in signatureHashMap, and if their combined stake meets the threshold, cause validate() to mark them CONSENSUS_REACHED and accept a stream file with an empty/invalid hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed once from the first signature's node and then reused for all hash groups, so that if the first node reports an artificially low totalStake, every subsequent hash group (including attacker-controlled ones) benefits from the lowered threshold, enabling a multi-transaction gossip attack across multiple stream files in a single batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in validate() by supplying a signature with a null node reference, since signature.getNode().getTotalStake() is called without a null check on getNode(), crashing the validation pipeline and preventing all subsequent stream files from being processed (denial of service)?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection containing a single VERIFIED signature from a node whose getStake() equals getTotalStake() (i.e., the node holds 100% of stake), causing canReachConsensus() to return true for any consensusRatio <= 1.0, and thereby allow a single-node consensus to accept any transaction the attacker controls on that node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap's behavior of allowing multiple values per key to insert many VERIFIED signatures with the same fileHashAsHex but different node objects that all report getStake() == 0, and because ConsensusNodeWrapper.getStake() returns 1 when totalStake == 0, accumulate enough count-based stake to meet stakeRequiredForConsensus without any real stake-weighted consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a totalStake value of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing BigDecimal.valueOf(Long.MAX_VALUE).multiply(consensusRatio).setScale(0, RoundingMode.CEILING) to produce a value that overflows when later compared against a long stake sum in canReachConsensus(), leading to incorrect consensus determination and potential acceptance of fraudulent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signatures collection where the first signature has status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), causing filename and totalStake to be set from a non-verified node, and then subsequent VERIFIED signatures use that potentially attacker-controlled totalStake for the consensus threshold computation, enabling threshold manipulation without controlling a VERIFIED signature's node data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() iterates all signatures to set filename/totalStake from the first one regardless of its status, then only adds VERIFIED ones to signatureHashMap, to inject a malicious first signature (any status) with a crafted node reporting a low totalStake, while the actual VERIFIED signatures from legitimate nodes use that manipulated threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause validate() to silently accept a stream file by supplying exactly one VERIFIED signature whose node's getStake() meets the stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold (e.g., when consensusRatio is 1/3 and the node holds exactly 1/3 of total stake), bypassing the intent of requiring multiple independent nodes to agree on the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic nature of the two-phase validate() logic (first loop builds signatureHashMap, second loop checks consensus) by supplying a collection that is modified concurrently between the two loops (if the collection is not thread-safe), causing the second loop to see a different set of signatures than the first, potentially bypassing the consensus check?
